title: Verilator -G affects all module's parameters
body:
I am learning to use verilator. For this purpose I coded a parameterized gray count counter. It was written in two styles.

The first version is synthesizable RTL which used a verilog function to generate the toggle bits of the counter. This version simulated in verilog without an issue. The file containing this counter is the following:

src/rtl/gray_counter_FN.sv

The second version is still synthezible but is written as structural code. There are sub modules for the gray counter first bit, the intermediate bits and a last bit. These submodules are interconnected in an N-bit gray counter using the System Verilog generate statement.

When simulating this code Verilator outputs only zeroes. Further debugging using the -Wall option of Verilator showed that the clock, reset and count signals of the counter are not used which implies that the instances of the gray code bits are not created. This code simulated with iverilog and VCS without any issue but produces only zeroes on Verilator.

clock reset count q (structural) q (rtl)
                 100 1 1           0 0 000000 000000  
                 200 1 1           0 0 000000 000000  
                 300 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 400 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 500 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 600 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 700 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 800 1 0           0 1 000000 000000  
                 900 1 0           1 1 000000 000001  
                1000 1 0           2 1 000000 000011  
                1100 1 0           3 1 000000 000010  
                1200 1 0           4 1 000000 000110  
                1300 1 0           5 1 000000 000111  
                1400 1 0           6 1 000000 000101  
                1500 1 0           7 1 000000 000100  
                1600 1 0           8 1 000000 001100 

You can see that the RTL code simulates correctly (it is counting in gray) but the structural code is stuck at zero.

The same code simulates correctly in iverilog (see that both counters count in gray).

clock reset count q 
                   0 1 1           0 0 xxxxxx xxxxxx  
                 100 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 200 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 300 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 400 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 500 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 600 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 700 1 0           0 0 000000 000000  
                 800 1 0           0 1 000000 000000  
                 900 1 0           1 1 000001 000001  
                1000 1 0           2 1 000011 000011  
                1100 1 0           3 1 000010 000010  
                1200 1 0           4 1 000110 000110  
                1300 1 0           5 1 000111 000111  
                1400 1 0           6 1 000101 000101  
                1500 1 0           7 1 000100 000100  
                1600 1 0           8 1 001100 001100  

The same code simulates correctly in iverilog.

Running verilator with the -Wall flags shows the problem. The instances are not generated and therefore clock, count and reset are not driving anything.

%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_wrapper.sv:20: Bits of signal are not used: q2[-1]
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_wrapper.sv:21: Bits of signal are not used: q3[-1]
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_wrapper.sv:22: Signal is not driven, nor used: q4
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_wrapper.sv:23: Signal is not driven, nor used: q5
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:11: Signal is not used: clock
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:12: Signal is not used: reset
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:13: Signal is not used: count
%Warning-UNDRIVEN: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:15: Signal is not driven: q
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:17: Bits of signal are not driven, nor used: zero[6:0]
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:17: Bits of signal are not used: zero[-1]
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:18: Bits of signal are not driven, nor used: toggle[6:0]
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_N.sv:18: Bits of signal are not used: toggle[-1]
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_FN.sv:17: Signal is not driven, nor used: zero
%Warning-UNUSED: src/rtl/gray_counter_FN.sv:18: Signal is not driven, nor used: toggle

Which explains why the structural code does not produce an output.

To reproduce the problem please untar the attached test case and do the following:

% make gray
% gray

Will run a verilator simulation which shows the problem

% make igray
% igray

Will run iverilog which works without a problem.

Thaks
Sergio

[gray.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/4519276/gray.tar.gz)
